title: Washing soda all-purpose spray
subtitle: A washing soda spray for kitchen and bathroom surfaces that degreases and disinfects
excerpt: Washing soda is sodium carbonate, a stronger alkali than bicarbonate of soda. Dissolved in warm water it is a powerful degreaser and general surface cleaner. A few drops of tea tree essential oil add mild antibacterial action. Do not use on aluminium, waxed surfaces, or natural stone.
sourceNotes: Washing soda cleaning properties from standard household chemistry references.

recipe.batchNotes: Make in larger quantities and decant into spray bottles.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready immediately once the washing soda is dissolved.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months. Washing soda solution is stable. Discard if the spray becomes cloudy or develops a smell.

recipeTools.notes: Washing soda is alkaline and can irritate skin with repeated contact

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium carbonate (Na2CO3). A natural mineral salt significantly more alkaline than bicarbonate of soda. It is a powerful degreaser and general surface cleaner. Do not use on aluminium (causes corrosion), waxed surfaces (strips wax), or natural stone (etches calcium-containing stone).

A cleaning product with a pH above 7. Alkaline solutions break down greasy, oily, and protein-based soils, which makes them effective kitchen and bathroom cleaners. Washing soda in water is pH 11 to 12.

An essential oil with well-documented antimicrobial properties, effective against a range of bacteria, yeasts, and moulds at concentrations of 0.5 to 2%. Added to cleaning sprays as a mild disinfectant boost.

content.text: dissolved in warm water is a straightforward and highly effective
for kitchen hobs, worktops, bathroom tiles, and sinks.

adds mild disinfectant action. Wear protective gloves for prolonged use; washing soda is alkaline enough to dry and irritate skin. Do not use on aluminium surfaces, waxed wood, natural stone, or painted surfaces not rated for alkaline cleaning. Shelf life is 6 months.

items.prepNote: Approximately 14 g; dissolved in the warm water

content.text: Dissolve washing soda in warm water and stir until completely clear. Allow to cool. Add tea tree essential oil and swirl to distribute. Pour into the spray bottle.

Store in the spray bottle. Shelf life is 6 months. Do not mix with acidic cleaners (vinegar, citric acid) in the same bottle; they neutralise each other. Do not use on aluminium, waxed surfaces, or natural stone.
